7 min read
Just now

--

On June 12, 2026, the AI world experienced something unprecedented. Anthropic, the company behind Claude, abruptly suspended access to its most powerful AI models — Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5 — for every user worldwide. No warning. No transition window. Just gone.
If you’re one of the millions of developers, researchers, or enterprises who woke up to an error message instead of your AI assistant, you’re probably asking the same questions I had: What just happened? Why? And when are they coming back?
Let me break down everything I’ve learned.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

(Image by )The Short Version
What happened: Anthropic suspended both Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 globally on June 12, 2026.
Why: The US government issued an export control directive citing national security concerns, requiring Anthropic to block access by any foreign national.
The trigger: The government claimed it found a “jailbreak” method that could bypass Fable 5’s safety safeguards.
Current status: Both models remain suspended. Anthropic is working to restore access “as soon as possible”.
What Are These Models, Anyway?
